title: add ProtocolError variant for server anomalies
description: WolfHsmError::Ffi{code:-1} is misused in ~7 places to signal server-side protocol anomalies: zero key IDs returned by wolfhsm_ecc_make_key/wolfhsm_rsa_make_key/wolfhsm_mldsa_make_key/wh_Client_KeyCache, unexpected output lengths from wolfhsm_cmac/wolfhsm_curve25519_shared_secret/wolfhsm_ed25519_sign, and erased key ID from wh_Client_CertVerifyAndCacheLeafPubKey. None of these are FFI errors (the FFI call returned 0). They are server-side protocol anomalies where the server returned success but the response payload is invalid. Suggested fix: add WolfHsmError::ProtocolError { msg: &'static str } variant and replace all Ffi{code:-1} uses at these sites. Sites: key.rs:66, rsa.rs:56, mldsa.rs:50 (zero key IDs); cmac.rs:57, curve25519.rs:71, ed25519.rs:68 (unexpected output lengths); cert.rs:167 (erased key ID post-verify).
notes: NEEDS HUMAN DECISION: adding ProtocolError variant is a public API change. Requires decision on whether to add before 0.1.0 publish.
